text.title: typescript-strict-mode Decision Tree

decision-tree.root-question: What kind of strict-mode error is this?

branch.question: Is the read part of an iteration loop or a single one-shot access?

branch.conclusion: Destructure with default: `const [first = fallback] = items;`. Or use a check `if (i < items.length) …`.

branch.question: Does the caller guarantee the value is present at this point?

branch.conclusion: Use an assertion function `assertDefined(x, 'must be loaded by middleware')` — NEVER `!`.
Optional chaining + default. Or refactor to a discriminated-union Result.
Add a type predicate `function isFoo(v: unknown): v is Foo { … }`. Or Zod-parse if the value crosses a trust boundary.

text.example: error: 'users[0]' is possibly 'undefined'. q-error-kind -> 'indexed-access-undefined' q-context -> 'single-access' => conclusion r2: `users[0]?.email ?? 'unknown@—'`.
